about.title: About MiaoMiaoWu
about.description: Learn about MiaoMiaoWu's design philosophy and core features

intro.name: MiaoMiaoWu
intro.text1: MiaoMiaoWu is a user management system designed specifically for self-hosted proxy services. It provides comprehensive node management, subscription generation, user management, and traffic statistics, allowing you to easily manage your own proxy services.
intro.text2: Additionally, MiaoMiaoWu supports taking over external subscriptions, enabling unified management and distribution of external subscription nodes alongside self-hosted ones.

designPhilosophy.heading: Design Philosophy
designPhilosophy.text: MiaoMiaoWu's design philosophy is simple, efficient, and secure. We hope users can:
designPhilosophy.item1: Quickly complete node management and subscription configuration through a clean interface
designPhilosophy.item2: Deploy and use without complex technical background
designPhilosophy.item3: Enjoy a secure and reliable proxy service management experience

nodeManagement.title: Node Management
nodeManagement.desc: Supports manual addition and external subscription import of nodes, with multiple protocols (VMess, VLESS, Trojan, Shadowsocks, etc.)

subscriptionGeneration.title: Subscription Generation
subscriptionGeneration.desc: Visual configuration of proxy groups and rules, with multiple templates, generating standard Clash format subscriptions

userManagement.title: User Management
userManagement.desc: Create and manage user accounts, assign different subscription configurations to different users

trafficStats.title: Traffic Statistics
trafficStats.desc: Integrated probe system, no additional components needed, monitors node traffic usage

customRules.title: Custom Rules
customRules.desc: Supports custom DNS configuration, rule lists, and rule-set providers

chainProxy.title: Chain Proxy
chainProxy.desc: Supports creating multi-layer proxy chains for relay acceleration and privacy protection

openSource.heading: Open Source
openSource.text: MiaoMiaoWu is an open-source project under the MIT license. Community contributions and issue submissions are welcome.

chainProxy.title: Chain Proxy
chainProxy.description: Forward traffic through multiple proxy servers

whatIs.heading: What is Chain Proxy
whatIs.desc: Chain Proxy is a technique that forwards traffic through multiple proxy servers to achieve more complex network routing strategies. MiaoMiaoWu implements chain proxy through dialer-proxy technology, allowing you to specify a relay node for any node to achieve multi-level proxy forwarding.
whatIs.example: Chain proxy means chaining multiple proxy nodes together so that traffic passes through multiple proxy servers before reaching the target website. For example:
whatIs.techDesc: In MiaoMiaoWu, this is implemented through Clash's dialer-proxy property. The source node forwards traffic to the specified target node (relay node), which then forwards it to the final destination.

scenarios.heading: Use Cases
scenarios.accelerateDesc: When the landing node is far from the user, a relay node closer to the user can accelerate the connection. For example: Domestic user → Hong Kong relay → US landing node.
scenarios.hideIpDesc: Hide the client's real IP address through multi-layer proxies for enhanced privacy protection. The target website can only see the IP of the last landing node.
scenarios.bypassDesc: Some services may restrict specific IP ranges. Chain proxy allows you to exit from nodes in different regions to bypass these restrictions.
scenarios.loadBalanceDesc: Distribute traffic across multiple relay nodes to reduce the load on a single node and improve overall stability.

configMethod.heading: Configuration Method
configMethod.desc: Configuring chain proxy in MiaoMiaoWu is very simple, just operate on the node management page:
configMethod.step1Desc: Click "Node Management" in the left menu
configMethod.step2Desc: Find the node that needs chain proxy configuration (landing node) in the node list
configMethod.step3Desc: Click the chain proxy icon (two arrows exchange icon) in the node action bar
configMethod.step4Desc: Select the relay node in the popup dialog
configMethod.step5Desc: After confirmation, the system will automatically create a new chain proxy node, labeled as "Chain Proxy"

groupConfig.heading: Proxy Group Configuration
groupConfig.desc: When generating subscriptions, MiaoMiaoWu supports configuring dedicated chain proxy groups:
groupConfig.landingDesc: Used to store the final exit nodes. The IPs of these nodes will be seen by the target website.
groupConfig.relayDesc: Used to store relay server nodes. Traffic passes through these nodes before being forwarded to landing nodes.
groupConfig.note: This grouping method is suitable for users with multiple landing nodes and multiple relay nodes, allowing flexible combination of different chains.

singleNode.heading: Single Node Configuration
singleNode.desc1: On the node management page, click the
singleNode.desc2: button to individually assign a relay node to a landing node. After assignment, a new chain proxy node will be created in the node table.
singleNode.demoNote: The above is an in-page simulation showing the "Relay Proxy Group" dropdown menu in the "Proxy Group Type Switch" button (not an image). In actual use, this configuration is written to the dialer-proxy-group field.

techPrinciple.heading: Technical Principle
techPrinciple.desc: Chain proxy implementation is based on Clash's dialer-proxy feature:
techPrinciple.explanation: The dialer-proxy property specifies the name of the relay proxy node. Clash first connects to the relay node, then connects to the target node through it.

notes.heading: Important Notes
notes.latencyDesc: : Chain proxy increases network latency because traffic needs to pass through multiple nodes
notes.stabilityDesc: : Any node failure in the chain will cause the entire chain to be unavailable
notes.bandwidthDesc: : The final speed is limited by the slowest node in the chain
notes.loopDesc: : Ensure chain configurations do not form circular references
notes.protocolDesc: : Ensure node protocols in the chain are compatible with each other

clientSetup.title: Client Setup
clientSetup.description: Import and use subscription links in various platform clients
clientSetup.tip: It is recommended to use clients that support Clash format for the best compatibility

generalSteps.heading: General Import Steps
generalSteps.step1: Get the subscription link from MiaoMiaoWu

clashVerge.step2: Click the "Subscriptions" menu on the left
clashVerge.step4: Paste the subscription link and save
clashVerge.step5: Click the refresh button to update the subscription

clashMeta.step3: Click the "+" button to add a profile
clashMeta.step4: Select "URL" type, paste the subscription link

shadowrocket.step2: Click the "+" button in the top right
shadowrocket.step5: Click done and update the subscription

customRules.title: Custom Rules
customRules.description: Create and manage custom routing rules (Admin feature)
customRules.intro: Custom rules allow administrators to create their own DNS configurations, rule lists, and rule-set providers, which can be used to override or supplement default rules in templates when generating subscriptions.

mainFeatures.heading: Main Features
mainFeatures.deleteDesc: Remove rule configurations that are no longer needed
mainFeatures.behaviorDesc: Supports three rule behaviors: domain, ipcidr, and classical

ruleTypes.heading: Rule Types
ruleTypes.desc: MiaoMiaoWu supports the following types of custom rules:
ruleTypes.rulesDesc: Directly define routing rules such as DOMAIN-SUFFIX, IP-CIDR, etc.
ruleTypes.ruleProvidersDesc: Use external rule-set providers, supporting both URL and file sources
ruleTypes.dnsDesc: Custom DNS server configuration such as nameserver, fallback, etc.

createSteps.heading: Creation Steps
createSteps.step1Desc: Click "Custom Rules" in the left menu
createSteps.step3Desc: Write rule configuration content in the editor
createSteps.step4Desc: Click the save button to complete rule creation
createSteps.ruleNameDesc: Set the display name of the rule
createSteps.ruleTypeDesc: Select the rule type (rules / rule-providers / DNS)
createSteps.ruleBehaviorDesc: Select the rule behavior (domain / ipcidr / classical)

referenceInSub.heading: Reference in Subscriptions
referenceInSub.desc: Created custom rules can be referenced when generating subscriptions to override or supplement default rules in templates.
referenceInSub.priorityDesc: Custom rules have higher priority than default rules in templates

scenarios.heading: Typical Scenarios
scenarios.directTitle: Direct Rules: Add domains or IPs that need direct connection
scenarios.streamingTitle: Streaming Routing: Create routing rules for specific streaming services
scenarios.adBlockTitle: Ad Blocking: Add blocking rules for ad domains
scenarios.lanTitle: LAN Rules: Configure direct connection rules for LAN addresses

notes.heading: Important Notes
notes.formatDesc: : Please ensure the rule format is correct; incorrect format may cause subscriptions to malfunction
notes.groupExistDesc: : Proxy group names referenced by rules type must exist in the subscription configuration

maintenance.heading: Rule Maintenance
maintenance.checkDesc: Regularly check whether rules are still valid and necessary
maintenance.feedbackDesc: Adjust and optimize rules based on user feedback

editNodes.title: Node & Proxy Group Editing
editNodes.description: Manage node assignments and proxy group configurations in subscriptions (Admin feature)
editNodes.intro: The node and proxy group editing feature allows administrators to add or remove nodes from generated subscriptions and configure proxy group types and ordering.

demo.heading: Operation Demo
demo.dragHint: Drag nodes to left proxy groups
demo.settingsHint: Click gear icon to configure proxy group

dragOps.heading: Drag & Drop Instructions
dragOps.dragNodeDesc: Drag nodes from right side into left proxy groups
dragOps.dragToAddDesc: Drag a node into a proxy group card to add it
dragOps.dragToRemoveDesc: Drag a node out of a proxy group to remove it
dragOps.dragCardDesc: Drag proxy group cards to reorder them
dragOps.clickTitleDesc: Click proxy group title to rename
dragOps.clickSettingsDesc: Click gear icon to configure proxy group type
dragOps.dragTitleDesc: Drag nodes to reorder within a proxy group
dragOps.dragAvailTitleDesc: Drag from right available nodes list into left proxy groups

notes.heading: Important Notes
notes.saveDesc: Save your changes promptly after editing; unsaved changes will be lost
notes.insertDesc: The order of nodes in proxy groups affects client display
notes.providerDesc: Proxy providers (purple border) and regular nodes can coexist in the same proxy group
notes.selfRefDesc: Proxy groups cannot reference themselves, as this would cause circular references

scenarios.heading: Related Features
scenarios.generatorDesc: Create and manage subscription configurations on the subscription generation page
scenarios.subscribeDesc: View and manage subscription files on the subscription management page

externalSubscriptions.title: External Subscriptions
externalSubscriptions.description: Import and manage external subscription links (Admin feature)
externalSubscriptions.intro: The external subscription feature allows administrators to import third-party subscription links, bringing external nodes into MiaoMiaoWu's unified management system.

step1.title: Go to External Subscriptions Page
step1.desc: Click "External Subscriptions" in the left menu

step2.title: Click the Add Button
step2.desc: Click the "Add Subscription" button on the page

step2b.title: Fill in Subscription Info
step2b.desc: Enter the subscription name and subscription link URL

step3.title: Select User-Agent (Optional)
step3.desc: Some subscription sources require a specific User-Agent to return nodes correctly

step4.title: Save and Sync
step4.desc: After saving, the system will automatically sync nodes from the subscription

manual.title: Manual Sync
manual.desc: Click the refresh button on the subscription card to manually trigger sync

force.title: Force Sync
force.desc: When enabled in system settings, the system automatically syncs external subscriptions every time a user fetches their subscription

trafficInfo.heading: Traffic Information
trafficInfo.desc: Some subscription sources return traffic usage information in response headers:

total.title: Total Traffic
total.desc: Total traffic quota of the subscription

notes.heading: Important Notes
notes.linkPrivacy: Subscription links contain sensitive information, please keep them safe
notes.userAgent: Some subscription sources have User-Agent requirements, please set accordingly
notes.trafficNote: Traffic information depends on whether the subscription source returns relevant data in response headers
notes.deleteNote: Deleting an external subscription will also delete all synced nodes

toNodes.title: External Subscriptions → Node Management
toNodes.desc: Synced nodes will appear in the node management list

toGenerator.title: External Subscriptions → Generate Subscription
toGenerator.descPrefix: Synced nodes can be used in

toProxyProviders.title: External Subscriptions → Proxy Providers
toProxyProviders.descPrefix: Can be automatically grouped by region or protocol through

toTraffic.title: External Subscriptions → Traffic Info
toTraffic.desc: After enabling traffic sync, external subscription traffic info will be merged into user traffic statistics

faq.title: FAQ
faq.description: Frequently asked questions about using MiaoMiaoWu

addNodes.a: You can add nodes in two ways: 1. Manual addition: Click the add button on the node management page and fill in node information; 2. External subscription: Add a subscription link on the external subscriptions page, and the system will automatically sync nodes.

importFailed.a: Please check if the subscription link is correct and the network is accessible. Some subscription sources may require a specific User-Agent. If it still fails, try adding nodes manually.

subNotShowing.q: What if users can't see subscriptions?
subNotShowing.a: Administrators need to assign subscriptions to users in "User Management". Users need at least one assigned subscription to see it on the "Subscription Links" page.

forgotAdminPwd.q: What if I forgot the admin password?
forgotAdminPwd.a: You can reset the admin password to default by adding the --reset-admin parameter when restarting the service.

supportedFormats.a: MiaoMiaoWu supports Clash/Meta format subscription output and can convert to 12 client formats through the built-in SubStore.

updateNodes.q: How to update external subscription nodes?
updateNodes.a: Click the refresh button on the external subscriptions page to manually sync. You can also enable auto-sync in system settings.

customRules.a: Create rules on the "Custom Rules" page, then apply them when editing configurations in "Generate Subscription" or "Subscription Management".

trafficStats.q: What if traffic statistics are inaccurate?
trafficStats.a: Please confirm the probe server is properly configured and the node is bound to the corresponding probe. If using external subscriptions, you need to enable traffic info sync in system settings.

chainProxy.a: Click the chain proxy button on a node in the node management page and select a relay node. For detailed instructions, refer to the chain proxy documentation.

backup.a: MiaoMiaoWu uses SQLite database, just back up the data directory. Docker users should back up the mounted volume directory; script installation users should back up the /etc/mmw directory.

help.heading: Need More Help?
help.desc: If none of the above questions resolve your issue, you can get help through:
help.reportBugDesc: If you found a bug, please submit an Issue on GitHub

forgotPassword.q: What if I forgot my password?
forgotPassword.a: Regular users should contact the administrator to reset their password

forgotAdminPassword.q: What if I forgot the admin password?
forgotAdminPassword.a: You can reset the admin password by adding the --reset-admin parameter when restarting the service

autoLogout.q: Why am I automatically logged out?
autoLogout.a: You need to log in again when the token expires. You can adjust the token validity period in system settings

overview.heading: Feature Overview
overview.desc: The node speed test feature allows administrators to test node latency, speed, and exit IP, supporting two testing methods:

local.title: Local Speed Test
local.desc: Test speed locally on the server using the mihomo core

home.title: Home Network Speed Test
home.desc: Test speed from a home network environment via mmw-agent

mihomo.heading: mihomo Core
mihomo.dep: Node speed test depends on the mihomo core
mihomo.detectOrder: The system auto-detects mihomo in this order: 1. Search in system PATH 2. Same directory as the executable
mihomo.manual: If auto-detection fails, you can manually specify the mihomo path

startTest.heading: Start Speed Test
startTest.step1: Go to the Node Management page
startTest.step4: Wait for the test to complete
startTest.collapseNote: You can collapse the speed test panel during testing

results.heading: Test Results
results.exitIp: Exit IP: Node's exit IP address and region

homeSetup.heading: Home Network Speed Test Setup
homeSetup.step1: Install mmw-agent on the home network machine
homeSetup.step2: Configure agent to connect to the MiaoMiaoWu server
homeSetup.step3: Initiate home network speed test from MiaoMiaoWu
homeSetup.note1: Home network speed test requires mmw-agent installation
homeSetup.note2: The agent needs to be able to access the MiaoMiaoWu server
homeSetup.note3: Test results are closer to actual usage experience

notes.heading: Important Notes
notes.serial: Speed tests are executed serially; many nodes will require longer waiting
notes.duration: Speed tests take approximately a few seconds to tens of seconds
notes.port: Ensure the port used by mihomo is not occupied

probe.title: Probe Management
probe.description: Manage probe servers and node bindings (Admin feature)

howItWorks.heading: How It Works
howItWorks.desc: The probe system collects traffic statistics by running probe programs on proxy servers:
howItWorks.flow1: Probe program runs on the proxy server
howItWorks.flow2: Collects traffic statistics in real time
howItWorks.flow3: Reports traffic data to MiaoMiaoWu periodically
howItWorks.flow4: MiaoMiaoWu aggregates and displays traffic statistics
howItWorks.flow5: Users view on the Traffic Info page
howItWorks.adv1Desc: Built-in probe system, no third-party monitoring needed
howItWorks.adv3Desc: Traffic data updated in real time
howItWorks.adv4Desc: Supports multiple probe servers running simultaneously

mainFeatures.heading: Main Features
mainFeatures.deleteDesc: Remove probe servers no longer in use
mainFeatures.bindDesc: Bind nodes to specific probe servers

configSteps.heading: Configuration Steps

configSteps.serverAddrDesc: Enter the probe server address and port

configSteps.step4Desc: Bind nodes to their corresponding probes on the Node Management page
configSteps.step5Desc: View traffic statistics on the Traffic Info page

trafficStats.heading: Traffic Statistics
trafficStats.desc: After binding probes, the system automatically collects node traffic usage:
trafficStats.boundDesc: Only nodes bound to probes have traffic statistics
trafficStats.bound1: Traffic is counted at the node level
trafficStats.bound2: Data is updated in real time
trafficStats.unboundDesc: Nodes not bound to probes do not participate in traffic statistics

notes.heading: Important Notes
notes.requirementDesc: Probe program must be installed and running on the proxy server

modeComparison.heading: Mode Comparison
modeComparison.desc: Each processing mode has its pros and cons; choose the one that suits you:
modeComparison.mmwDesc: Nodes are processed server-side and embedded in the subscription file, suitable for most scenarios
modeComparison.clientDesc: Retains proxy-provider configuration for the client to pull directly, suitable for scenarios requiring real-time updates

modeSuggestions.heading: Mode Selection Guide
modeSuggestions.mmw4: Does not expose original subscription URL
modeSuggestions.client3: Need client to manage nodes directly

example.heading: Configuration Example
example.step2Desc2: drag proxy providers into proxy groups

notes.heading: Important Notes
notes.settingDesc: : "Enable Proxy Providers" must be turned on in System Settings
notes.perfDesc: : Client processing mode may increase client memory usage
notes.compatDesc: : Not all clients support the proxy-provider feature
notes.geoipDesc: : Region filtering depends on region keywords in node names

quickStart.title: Quick Start
quickStart.description: Get started quickly with MiaoMiaoWu proxy subscription management system

step1.heading: Step 1: Installation
step1.desc: Choose a suitable installation method to deploy MiaoMiaoWu:

accessSystem.title: Access the System
accessSystem.desc: Open the MiaoMiaoWu URL in your browser

initAdmin.title: Create Admin
initAdmin.desc: First visit automatically redirects to the admin creation page

login.title: Log In
login.desc: Log in with the admin account you created

step3.heading: Step 3: Add Nodes
step3.desc: Add proxy nodes using the following methods:

manual.title: Manual Addition
manual.desc: Manually enter node information on the Node Management page

subImport.title: Subscription Import
subImport.desc: Import subscription links on the External Subscriptions page

goToPage.title: Go to Generate Subscription Page
goToPage.desc: Click "Generate Subscription" in the left menu

selectTemplate.title: Select Template
selectTemplate.desc: Choose a built-in or custom template

configGroups.title: Configure Proxy Groups
configGroups.desc: Adjust proxy groups and node assignments as needed

save.title: Save Subscription
save.desc: Enter a name and save the subscription configuration

createUser.title: Create User
createUser.desc: Create new users on the User Management page

getLink.title: Get Link
getLink.desc: Users can get their subscription after logging in on the Subscription Links page

complete.heading: Done!
complete.desc: Congratulations! You have completed the basic MiaoMiaoWu setup. Now you can explore more advanced features:

settings.title: User Settings
settings.description: Manage personal account information and preferences
settings.intro: The User Settings page allows users to manage their personal information, including modifying nickname, email, and password. All users can access this page.

mainFeatures.heading: Main Features
mainFeatures.nicknameDesc: Update the nickname displayed in the system
mainFeatures.emailDesc: Update the email address associated with the account
mainFeatures.twoFaDesc: Enable TOTP verification code for enhanced account security
mainFeatures.debugDesc: Temporarily enable debug logging for troubleshooting

settingsPage.heading: Settings Page
settingsPage.usernameNote: Username cannot be changed after creation

editInfo.heading: Edit Personal Information
editInfo.step1Title: Click the user avatar in the top right
editInfo.step2Desc: Go to the User Settings page
editInfo.step3Desc: Fill in the nickname or email you want to change
editInfo.step4Desc: Click the "Save Information" button to complete

changePwd.heading: Change Password
changePwd.step2Desc: Use a strong password with uppercase, lowercase letters, numbers, and special characters
changePwd.step3Desc: Enter the new password again to ensure consistency
changePwd.step4Desc: Click the "Change Password" button; you will need to log in again after success

twoFa.heading: Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)
twoFa.desc: Two-factor authentication adds an extra security layer to your account. Once enabled, you'll need a dynamic verification code in addition to your password when logging in.
twoFa.setup1: 1. Find the "Two-Factor Authentication" section on the settings page, click "Set up 2FA"
twoFa.setup2: 2. Scan the QR code with an authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Microsoft Authenticator, etc.)
twoFa.setup3: 3. Enter the 6-digit code shown in the app to complete binding
twoFa.setup4: 4. Securely save the recovery codes for account access if the authenticator is lost
twoFa.recoveryDesc:
• One-time recovery codes are generated when enabling 2FA
• Use recovery codes to log in when your phone is lost or the authenticator is unavailable
• Be sure to save recovery codes in a safe place; they are shown only once

debugLog.heading: Debug Log
debugLog.desc: The debug log feature is used for temporarily collecting detailed runtime logs to help troubleshoot subscription fetching, node sync, and other issues.
debugLog.usage1: After clicking "Enable Debug", the system starts recording detailed logs
debugLog.usage2: Debug mode automatically closes after 5 minutes to prevent log files from growing too large
debugLog.usage3: You can manually close debug mode at any time
debugLog.usage4: Supports downloading log files or viewing the latest logs in real time
debugLog.usage5: Log files are automatically cleaned up after 7 days

notes.heading: Important Notes
notes.usernameDesc: Username cannot be changed after creation; only the nickname can be modified
notes.passwordDesc: Recommended password length of at least 8 characters, including letters and numbers
notes.afterChangeDesc: The current session will be invalidated after a password change; you need to log in again
notes.forgotDesc: If you forgot your current password, contact the administrator to reset it
notes.twoFaDesc: After enabling two-factor authentication, be sure to save recovery codes; administrator assistance is needed if lost

subscribeFiles.title: Subscription Management
subscribeFiles.description: Manage subscription file versions and user assignments (Admin feature)
subscribeFiles.intro: The Subscription Management page is an admin-only feature for managing subscription file versions, viewing subscription details, and assigning subscription configurations to users. It supports multi-version management and user subscription assignment.

mainFeatures.heading: Main Features
mainFeatures.viewListDesc: : View all created subscription files and their basic information
mainFeatures.sortDesc: : Support drag-and-drop sorting to adjust the display order of subscriptions
mainFeatures.importDesc: : Directly import external subscriptions with ability to edit external subscription rules
mainFeatures.uploadDesc: : Upload locally used subscription files to generate subscription links
mainFeatures.generateDesc: : Same as the Generate Subscription menu
mainFeatures.applyRulesDesc: : Use custom rules to override configuration file rules
mainFeatures.editNodesDesc: : Add or remove nodes from generated subscriptions. For detailed operations, refer to
mainFeatures.bindV3Desc: : Bind subscription to a V3 template; subscription configuration will be fully generated based on the template, and new nodes are automatically added
mainFeatures.tagFilterDesc: : After binding a template, filter nodes by tags; only nodes matching the tags will be added to the subscription
mainFeatures.customLinkDesc: : Set a custom short code for subscriptions to replace auto-generated short link codes, making subscription links easier to remember

sorting.heading: Subscription Sorting
sorting.desc: The subscription list supports drag-and-drop sorting to adjust display order:
sorting.methodDescPrefix: Click the drag icon on the left side of each row (
sorting.methodDescSuffix: ), hold and drag to change the subscription order. Sort results are saved automatically.

layout.heading: Edit Page Layout
layout.desc: Uses a two-column layout for convenient node dragging:
layout.leftDesc: Shows all proxy group cards from the configuration file
layout.rightDesc: Shows all available nodes from the node table

versionMgmt.heading: Version Management
versionMgmt.desc: Subscription files support multi-version management. Each time you edit and save a subscription configuration in "Generate Subscription", the system automatically creates a new version.
versionMgmt.versionNumberDesc: : Each version has a unique version number, incrementing by creation time
versionMgmt.createTimeDesc: : Records when the version was created
versionMgmt.currentVersionDesc: : Identifies the version currently used by users
versionMgmt.rollbackDesc: If a new version has issues, you can roll back to a previous stable version. This feature is under development.

bindV3.heading: Bind V3 Template
bindV3.desc: Subscriptions can be bound to V3 templates. Once bound, subscription configuration will be fully generated based on the template, enabling automatic node management.
bindV3.methodDesc: Click the "Bind Template" button in the subscription list and select the V3 template to bind.
bindV3.effect1: Subscription configuration is fully generated based on the template; existing rule configuration will be ignored
bindV3.effect2: When new nodes are added, if they match the template's filter rules, they are automatically added to the subscription
bindV3.effect3: When nodes are deleted, they are automatically removed from the subscription
bindV3.effect4: When the template is modified, subscriptions bound to it are automatically regenerated

tagFilter.heading: Tag Filtering
tagFilter.desc: After binding a V3 template, you can further filter nodes by tags. Only nodes matching the tags will be added to the subscription.
tagFilter.usage1: Select tags to filter in subscription details
tagFilter.usage2: Supports selecting multiple tags; a node only needs to match one tag
tagFilter.usage3: When no tags are selected, all nodes from the template are used
tagFilter.scenarioDesc: Assign nodes with different tags to different users. For example: VIP users get nodes tagged "High Speed", regular users get nodes tagged "Standard".

typicalScenario.heading: Typical Use Case
typicalScenario.newNodeDesc: Click Edit Nodes, and drag the new nodes from available nodes to the left proxy groups. For detailed operations, refer to

relations.heading: Feature Relations
relations.desc: Subscription Management is closely related to other feature modules, forming a complete subscription service workflow:
relations.genToSubDesc: After creating or editing subscription configurations in "Generate Subscription", view version history in "Subscription Management"
relations.subToUserDesc: You can quickly navigate from "User Management" to view a specific user's subscription assignments
relations.nodeToSubDesc: Subscription configurations reference nodes from "Node Management"; node changes affect subscription content
relations.subToLinkDesc: The subscriptions users see on the "Subscription Links" page are the ones administrators have assigned to them

notes.heading: Important Notes
notes.nodeModDesc: : When nodes are edited in Node Management, changes are automatically synced to subscriptions
notes.deleteDesc: : Deleting a subscription file in "Generate Subscription" will also delete all versions and user assignments

subscriptionLink.title: Subscription Links
subscriptionLink.description: Get and manage your subscription links

mainFeatures.heading: Main Features
mainFeatures.intro: The Subscription Links page provides convenient subscription management features, supporting subscription link generation and import for multiple client formats.
mainFeatures.cardDisplayDesc: : Shows all subscription configurations assigned to you by the administrator, including subscription name, description, update time, and version number
mainFeatures.viewLinkDesc: : Each subscription card displays the full subscription link URL, supporting direct copy
mainFeatures.multiClientDesc: : Click the "Copy" button to select different client formats (12 clients)
mainFeatures.importClashDesc: : Click the "Import Clash" button to directly open and import the subscription in a Clash client
mainFeatures.qrCodeDesc: : Click the subscription icon to display a QR code for easy mobile device scanning and import

preview.heading: Interface Preview
preview.desc: Here is an example of a subscription card:
preview.cardDesc: Includes Hong Kong, Japan, Singapore nodes

steps.heading: Usage Steps
steps.step1Desc: Select the subscription configuration you want to use from the subscription card list
steps.step2PcDesc: : Click the "Copy" button, select the corresponding client format
steps.step2MobileDesc: : Click the QR code icon, scan with your mobile client to import
steps.step2ClashDesc: : Click the "Import Clash" button to open directly
steps.step3Desc: Follow client prompts to complete subscription import and configuration update

clientFormats.heading: Supported Client Formats
clientFormats.desc: Subscription links support automatic conversion to the following 12 client formats:

notes.heading: Important Notes
notes.note1: Subscription links contain your personal authentication information; do not share with others
notes.note2: If the subscription list is empty, the administrator has not assigned subscriptions to you yet; please contact the administrator
notes.note3: Client conversion is implemented via SubStore. If you encounter issues, contact the developer or submit an Issue
notes.note4: It is recommended to regularly update subscriptions to get the latest node configurations and rules
notes.note5: Some providers frequently change domains and IPs; you can enable forced external subscription sync in System Settings

systemRequirements.title: System Requirements
systemRequirements.description: Server and client requirements for MiaoMiaoWu

browser.heading: Browser Requirements
browser.desc: MiaoMiaoWu admin panel supports all modern browsers:

systemSettings.title: System Settings
systemSettings.intro: The System Settings page is an admin-only feature for configuring system-level global settings, including external subscription sync, feature switches, and other core function configurations.

externalSync.heading: External Subscription Sync Settings
externalSync.desc: Configure the sync behavior of external subscription links, controlling node data update strategies and caching mechanisms.
externalSync.trafficDesc:
• Enabled: Traffic information data includes external subscription traffic info
• Disabled: Only counts traffic for locally managed nodes
externalSync.syncDesc:
• Enabled: Every time a user fetches their subscription link, the system re-fetches the latest node data from external subscription sources
• Disabled: Uses cached external subscription data without real-time updates
• Note: Enabling increases subscription API response time
externalSync.matchDesc: When external subscription sync is enabled, you can choose the node matching method:
externalSync.matchNameDesc: : Match and update node information based on node name; suitable for subscription sources with stable node names
externalSync.matchServerDesc: : Match based on server address and port; suitable for subscription sources with frequently changing node names
externalSync.matchTypeDesc: : Match based on protocol type, server address, and port; the strictest matching method
externalSync.scopeSavedDesc: : Only update nodes already in the database; do not add new nodes
externalSync.scopeAllDesc: : Sync all nodes from external subscriptions, including new ones
externalSync.keepNameDesc:
• Enabled: Keep existing node names in the database during sync; do not use external subscription node names
• Disabled: Overwrite local names with external subscription node names
externalSync.cacheDesc:
• Set to 0: Re-fetch external subscription nodes every time a subscription is fetched (real-time updates)
• Greater than 0: Only re-fetch when the time since last sync exceeds the set minutes
• Warning: Setting to 0 affects subscription API response speed
externalSync.excludeDesc:
• Purpose: Automatically exclude nodes whose names match specified keywords when syncing external subscriptions
• Default: remaining|traffic|expiry|subscription|time|reset
• Format: Separate multiple keywords with |, supports regular expressions

featureSwitches.heading: Feature Switches
featureSwitches.desc: Manage the enabled status of system features; turn on or off as needed.
featureSwitches.probeOn2: You can bind each node to a specific probe server
featureSwitches.probeOn3: Traffic statistics only aggregate traffic from probe servers with bound nodes
featureSwitches.probeOff2: Traffic statistics aggregate traffic from all probe servers
featureSwitches.shortLinkOn1: Subscription links page shows 6-character short links
featureSwitches.shortLinkOn3: Short links can be reset in "Personal Settings"
featureSwitches.shortLinkOff1: Only full subscription links are shown
featureSwitches.shortLinkTip: Tip: Short links are easier to share and remember, and do not expose user ID information
featureSwitches.newTemplateOn2: Supports managing templates via web interface (create, edit, delete)
featureSwitches.newTemplateOn3: Template data is backed up with the database
featureSwitches.newTemplateOff1: Uses template files in the rule_templates directory
featureSwitches.newTemplateOff2: Templates must be managed by manually editing files
featureSwitches.newTemplateOff3: Suitable for scenarios requiring version control of templates
featureSwitches.proxyProviderOn1: Subscription file page shows "Proxy Provider" management features
featureSwitches.proxyProviderOn2: You can create Proxy Provider configurations
featureSwitches.proxyProviderOn3: In "Node & Proxy Group Editing", you can drag proxy providers into proxy groups
featureSwitches.proxyProviderOn4: Proxy providers are shown with purple borders to distinguish from regular nodes
featureSwitches.proxyProviderOff1: Proxy provider related features are not shown
featureSwitches.proxyProviderTip: Tip: Proxy Providers allow dynamically loading nodes from external subscriptions, ideal for managing multiple providers
featureSwitches.subInfoOn1: Subscription output adds expiry time and remaining traffic info nodes at the top of the node list
featureSwitches.subInfoOn2: Client node list can directly show subscription status info
featureSwitches.subInfoOn3: Customizable expiry time prefix and remaining traffic prefix
featureSwitches.subInfoOff1: Subscription output does not include info nodes
featureSwitches.silentOn1: All non-subscription API requests return a 404 page
featureSwitches.silentOn2: After fetching a subscription, users get a temporary access window (configurable timeout)
featureSwitches.silentOn3: After timeout, returns 404 again until the user re-fetches the subscription
featureSwitches.silentOff1: Admin panel and all pages display normally
featureSwitches.silentTimeout1: Default 15 minutes; users can normally access the admin panel within this time after fetching a subscription
featureSwitches.silentTimeout2: Automatically returns to silent state after timeout
featureSwitches.silentWarning: Security feature: Silent mode effectively prevents panel scanning and probing, suitable for high-security scenarios
featureSwitches.clientCompatOn1: Automatically filters incompatible nodes based on the client type in user requests
featureSwitches.clientCompatOn2: Ensures clients only receive nodes with supported protocols and transport methods
featureSwitches.clientCompatOff1: Returns all nodes without compatibility filtering
featureSwitches.responseHeaderOn1: HTTP response headers of the subscription API carry traffic usage information
featureSwitches.responseHeaderOn2: Supported clients (Clash, Stash, etc.) can display remaining traffic and expiry time in the UI
featureSwitches.responseHeaderOff1: Response headers do not carry traffic information
featureSwitches.overrideScriptOn2: Scripts execute when subscriptions are fetched and can modify node configurations
featureSwitches.overrideScriptOn3: Supports two hooks: post_fetch and pre_save_nodes
featureSwitches.overrideScriptOn4: Script execution order and enable/disable status can be configured
featureSwitches.customSubLinkDesc: Administrators can set custom short codes for subscriptions and users on the "Subscription File Management" and "User Management" pages:
featureSwitches.customSubLink1Desc: : Replaces auto-generated subscription file short code; only letters and numbers supported
featureSwitches.customSubLink3: Custom short codes are used in short link generation, making subscription links easier to remember

notification.heading: Notification System
notification.desc: Receive system event notifications via Telegram Bot. You need to configure the Bot Token and Chat ID first.
notification.enableDesc: : Master switch; no notifications are sent when disabled
notification.chatIdDesc: : Chat ID to receive notifications (personal or group)
notification.testDesc: After configuration, click "Send Test Notification" to verify
notification.eventsDesc: Each event notification can be individually enabled or disabled:
notification.subFetchDesc: : Notify when users fetch subscription links
notification.ipBanDesc: : Notify when brute-force attempts are detected and IPs are banned
notification.silentDesc: : Notify when silent mode status changes
notification.dailyDesc: : Send daily traffic statistics summary at a scheduled time
notification.expiryDesc: : Notify when user subscriptions are about to expire
notification.dailyTimeDesc:
• Format: HH:MM, e.g., 08:00
• The system automatically sends the previous day's traffic statistics at the specified time

typicalScenarios.heading: Typical Use Scenarios
typicalScenarios.s1Desc: Administrator imports external subscription links as node sources → Enable "External Subscription Sync Settings" → Set appropriate cache time (e.g., 30 minutes) → Select match rules → Node info stays up to date each time users fetch subscriptions.
typicalScenarios.s2Desc: Add probe servers in "Probe Management" → Enable "Node Probe Server Binding" in system settings → Bind each node to its corresponding probe → Achieve per-node precise traffic statistics.
typicalScenarios.s3Desc: Enable "Short Links" → Users can use 6-character short links → Easy to share and remember → Can be reset in personal settings if needed.
typicalScenarios.s4Desc: Enable "Proxy Providers" → Create proxy providers to split nodes by region → Use proxy providers in proxy groups → Easy to switch between different providers' nodes.
typicalScenarios.s5Desc: Enable "Silent Mode" → Set timeout to 15 minutes → Enable "IP Ban Notification" → Panel only responds to subscription requests externally, effectively preventing scanning and probing.
typicalScenarios.s6Desc: Configure Telegram Bot → Enable "Daily Traffic Report" and "Subscription Expiry Notification" → Stay informed about system status and user conditions.

notes.heading: Important Notes
notes.perfDesc: : Setting cache time to 0 significantly increases subscription API response time
notes.matchDesc: : If external subscription node names change frequently, use "Server:Port" matching
notes.probeDesc: : Add probe servers in "Probe Management" before using probe features
notes.shortLinkDesc: : After resetting short links, old short links will become invalid
notes.templateDesc: : After switching template systems, confirm that the templates in use are correct
notes.silentDesc: : When enabled, all non-subscription requests return 404; make sure you know how to restore access by fetching a subscription
notes.scriptDesc: : Scripts execute when subscriptions are fetched; coding errors may cause abnormal subscription output
notes.immediateDesc: : All setting changes take effect immediately without restarting the service

bestPractices.heading: Best Practices
bestPractices.cacheDesc: : Set cache time based on node update frequency; 15-60 minutes is recommended
bestPractices.onDemandDesc: : Only enable features you actually need to avoid unnecessary complexity
bestPractices.newTemplateDesc: : Database templates are recommended for easy web-based management
bestPractices.shortLinkDesc: : Recommended to enable short links for better user experience
bestPractices.checkDesc: : Regularly check if external subscription sync is working and probe servers are online

templates.title: Template Management
templates.description: Manage subscription configuration templates for quick subscription generation
templates.intro: The Template Management page is an admin-only feature for managing subscription configuration templates. Templates enable quick generation of standardized subscription configuration files.

systemSwitch.heading: Template System Switch
systemSwitch.desc: MiaoMiaoWu supports two template systems, which can be switched in
systemSwitch.descSuffix: via the "Use New Template System" toggle:
systemSwitch.newDesc: Uses database-stored templates with web-based management support. The template format is compatible with subscription conversion backends like subconverter, so you can directly use templates shared on GitHub.
systemSwitch.oldDesc: Uses template files in the rule_templates directory. Templates are complete Clash configuration files (without proxies), requiring manual file editing for management.

newTemplate.heading: New Templates (Database Templates) Template V2
newTemplate.desc: The new template system uses a format fully compatible with various subscription conversion backends (subconverter, sub-web, etc.). You can directly use templates shared on GitHub:
newTemplate.formatDesc: New templates use INI format with the following main sections:
newTemplate.exampleTitle: Click to view new template example (INI format)

oldTemplate.heading: Old Templates (File Templates) - Template V1
oldTemplate.desc: Old templates are stored in the server's rule_templates directory as complete Clash configuration files but without proxies configuration; nodes are automatically populated when generating subscriptions.
oldTemplate.formatDesc: Old templates use YAML format with the following main sections:
oldTemplate.exampleTitle: Click to view old template example (YAML format)

mainFeatures.heading: Main Features
mainFeatures.createDesc: Create a new configuration template from an external template URL (new template system)
mainFeatures.editDesc: Modify template name, URL, and other configuration
mainFeatures.deleteDesc: Remove templates that are no longer in use
mainFeatures.loadDesc: Quickly load template configuration when generating subscriptions

builtIn.heading: Built-in Templates
builtIn.desc: MiaoMiaoWu includes multiple commonly used configuration templates ready to use:
builtIn.acl4ssrDesc: Configuration templates based on ACL4SSR rules, providing various rule set combinations:
builtIn.acl4ssrFullNoAuto: : Full version without auto speed test

createSteps.heading: Template Creation Steps
createSteps.newStep1: Click the "New Template" button on the "Generate Subscription" page
createSteps.newStep2: Fill in the template name and template URL (e.g., a GitHub template link)
createSteps.newStep3: Enable "Use Proxy" option if the template URL requires proxy access
createSteps.newStep4: Click save to complete template creation
createSteps.oldStep1: Create a YAML file in the server's rule_templates directory
createSteps.oldStep2: Write template content in Clash configuration format (keep proxies field empty)
createSteps.oldStep3: Refresh the page to see the new template on the "Generate Subscription" page

useTemplate.heading: Using Templates
useTemplate.desc: Created templates can be used on the "Generate Subscription" page:
useTemplate.step2Desc: Choose the template from the dropdown menu
useTemplate.step3Desc: The system will load configuration from the template URL and apply it
useTemplate.step4Desc: Adjust proxy groups and node assignments as needed
useTemplate.step5Desc: Enter the subscription name and save

notes.heading: Important Notes
notes.formatDesc: : New templates use INI format (subconverter format), old templates use YAML format (Clash configuration); they cannot be mixed
notes.urlDesc: : Ensure the template URL is accessible, otherwise loading will fail
notes.proxyDesc: : If the template URL is blocked, enable the "Use Proxy" option
notes.switchDesc: : After switching template systems in "System Settings", previously created templates are retained, but only templates of the current system are displayed
notes.deleteDesc: : Deleting a template does not affect subscriptions already generated using that template

templatesV3.title: Template Management V3
templatesV3.description: Advanced template system with mihomo-style subscription configuration management
templatesV3.intro: Template Management V3 is MiaoMiaoWu's latest template system, featuring mihomo-style proxy group configuration syntax with support for include-all, filter, and other advanced features. It solves the problem of manually editing subscriptions when adding nodes by automatically adding nodes to subscriptions based on templates.

prerequisite.title: Prerequisite
prerequisite.desc1: You need to enable "Use New Template System" and select "V3 Version Template" in
prerequisite.desc2: for the template management menu to appear.

versionCompare.heading: Template Version Comparison
versionCompare.desc1: MiaoMiaoWu supports three template versions, which can be switched in
versionCompare.v1Desc: Uses YAML files in the rule_templates directory. These are complete Clash configuration files; nodes are auto-populated into the proxies field during generation.
versionCompare.v2Desc: Uses INI format (subconverter format), defining proxy groups via custom_proxy_group. Compatible with various subscription conversion templates on GitHub.
versionCompare.v3Desc: Uses YAML format with mihomo's include-all, filter, and other advanced syntax. Provides a visual editing interface and supports automatic regional proxy group generation.

coreConcepts.heading: Core Concepts
coreConcepts.desc: V3 templates are based on mihomo's proxy group syntax, using declarative configuration to automatically match and assign nodes:
coreConcepts.includeAll: Include all nodes and proxy providers

configAttrs.heading: Proxy Group Configuration Attributes
configAttrs.typeDesc: Proxy group type: select, url-test, fallback, load-balance, relay
configAttrs.proxiesDesc: Static proxy list (other proxy group names, DIRECT/REJECT, or placeholders like __PROXY_NODES__)
configAttrs.iconDesc: Proxy group icon, can be an image URL or emoji
configAttrs.hiddenDesc: Whether to hide this proxy group in the client (default false)
configAttrs.includeAllDesc: Include all outbound proxies and proxy providers
configAttrs.includeAllProxiesDesc: Include all outbound proxy nodes only
configAttrs.includeTypeDesc: Include by node type, separate multiple types with |
configAttrs.includeRegionDesc: Include preset regional proxy groups (MiaoMiaoWu extension)
configAttrs.filterDesc: Filter nodes matching regex, separate multiple with `
configAttrs.excludeFilterDesc: Exclude nodes matching regex, separate multiple with `
configAttrs.excludeTypeDesc: Exclude by node type, separate multiple types with |
configAttrs.dialerProxyDesc: Specify relay proxy group name; traffic is forwarded through this group before reaching the target (chain proxy)
configAttrs.dialerProxyNote: After setting a relay proxy group, traffic from nodes in this proxy group is first forwarded through the selected node in the relay proxy group, achieving Client → Relay Node → Landing Node → Target chain proxy effect. This can be quickly set via the link icon on the right side of proxy groups in the visual editor.
configAttrs.yamlVarDesc: V3 templates support defining custom variables at the top level to avoid repeating complex regex in multiple proxy groups:
configAttrs.yamlVar1: Define non-Clash standard string fields at the YAML top level to use as variables
configAttrs.yamlVar2: Proxy group filter and exclude-filter can directly reference variable names
configAttrs.yamlVar3: Custom variables are automatically removed from the final generated configuration
configAttrs.yamlVarExample: Click to view YAML variable example
configAttrs.proxyGroupExample: Click to view proxy group configuration example
configAttrs.equivDesc: Equivalent to the following page configuration:
configAttrs.resultExample: Click to view proxy group subscription result

nodeTypes.heading: Supported Node Types
nodeTypes.desc: include-type and exclude-type support the following node types (case-insensitive):

regionGroups.heading: Regional Proxy Groups
regionGroups.desc: MiaoMiaoWu V3 templates support automatic generation of regional proxy groups. Enable the "Regional Proxy Groups" switch when editing a template. The system automatically matches regions based on node names and creates corresponding proxy groups.
regionGroups.usage1: After enabling the regional proxy groups switch, all regional proxy groups can be included
regionGroups.usage2: Regional proxy groups automatically match based on region keywords in node names
regionGroups.usage3: The "Other Regions" group includes all nodes not matched to specific regions
regionGroups.usage4: Regional proxy groups with no matched nodes are automatically removed from subscriptions; no need to delete unused ones from the template

createMethods.heading: Template Creation Methods
createMethods.uploadDesc: Upload a local YAML template file; the filename becomes the template name.
createMethods.pasteDesc: Directly paste YAML format template content; enter the template name and save.
createMethods.convertDesc: Convert existing V2 (subconverter INI format) templates to V3 format. The system automatically parses custom_proxy_group and generates corresponding proxy group configurations.
createMethods.fromSubDesc: Analyze proxy groups in existing subscription configurations, automatically generate filter regex and include-all configuration, and quickly create templates matching the existing subscription structure.
createMethods.blankDesc: Create a blank template with basic configuration, then add proxy groups through the visual editor.

visualEditor.heading: Visual Editor
visualEditor.desc: V3 templates provide a visual editing interface; no need to manually write YAML code to configure proxy groups:
visualEditor.desktopDesc: : Three-column layout, proxy group editor on the left, configuration preview on the right
visualEditor.tabletDesc: : Two-column layout, editor and preview each take half
visualEditor.mobileDesc: : Single-column layout, preview shows below the save button
visualEditor.filterKeywordDesc: : Enter keywords to auto-generate filter regex
visualEditor.excludeKeywordDesc: : Enter keywords to auto-generate exclude-filter regex
visualEditor.nodeTypeDesc: : Popup multi-select for choosing node types to include/exclude
visualEditor.includeOptionsDesc: : One-click enable include-all, include-all-proxies, and other options
visualEditor.iconHiddenDesc: : Configure proxy group icon and set whether to hide it
visualEditor.groupRefDesc: : Select and drag to sort other proxy groups
visualEditor.relayDesc: : Click the link icon to set relay proxy group for chain proxy
visualEditor.previewDesc: : Right side shows generated YAML configuration in real time
visualEditor.visualModeDesc: : Configure proxy groups through forms, ideal for quick editing
visualEditor.yamlModeDesc: : Edit YAML code directly, ideal for advanced users
visualEditor.modeSyncDesc: You can switch between modes at any time; data syncs automatically

subBinding.heading: Subscription Binding
subBinding.desc: V3 templates can be bound to subscriptions. Once bound, subscription configuration will be fully generated based on the template:
subBinding.flow1: Select the subscription to bind on the Subscription Management page
subBinding.flow2: Select the V3 template to bind
subBinding.flow3: System automatically regenerates subscription configuration based on template rules
subBinding.behavior1: Subscription configuration is fully generated based on the template; existing rule configuration will be ignored
subBinding.behavior2: Matches all nodes in the node table based on proxy group filter rules in the template
subBinding.behavior3: Matched nodes are added to proxy group proxies lists and top-level proxies
subBinding.behavior4: Template include-*, filter, exclude-* attributes are removed during generation
subBinding.behavior5: A subscription can only bind to one template
subBinding.autoUpdate1: When new nodes are added, if proxy group rules match new nodes, bound subscriptions are automatically updated
subBinding.autoUpdate2: When nodes are deleted, they are automatically removed from template-bound subscriptions
subBinding.autoUpdate3: When the template is modified, subscriptions bound to it are automatically regenerated

commonScenarios.heading: Common Usage Scenarios
commonScenarios.desc: Below are several typical configuration examples. Copy them into your template directly, or combine them. Each scenario shows the config snippet and the expected behavior.
commonScenarios.s1Title: Scenario 1: Auto-sync new nodes into subscriptions (most common)
commonScenarios.s1Goal: When an admin adds a new node, every subscription bound to this template automatically picks it up. No manual subscription editing required. This is V3 template's core power.
commonScenarios.s1Config: Declare include-all on the proxy group (pulls all outbound proxies + providers), or include-all-proxies (pulls only proxies). Miaomiaowu auto-injects matching nodes into proxies on subscription generation.
commonScenarios.s1Effect: Every new node added to the node table is instantly synced into all subscriptions bound to this template. Same for deletion — auto-removed. No manual regen needed.
commonScenarios.s2Title: Scenario 2: Group by region (auto-recognise HK/JP/US etc.)
commonScenarios.s2Goal: Want subscriptions to automatically contain 'HK nodes', 'JP nodes', 'US nodes' groups, and new nodes auto-classified into their region group.
commonScenarios.s2Config: Enable the Region Proxy Groups switch in the Edit Template dialog — Miaomiaowu auto-detects region from node name keywords (HK / Hong Kong / 香港 etc.). Or write filter manually: filter: 'HK|香港|Hong' per group.
commonScenarios.s2Effect: A new node named 'HK-04' → auto-joins the 'HK nodes' group. The 'Other Regions' group catches anything not matched (fallback). Region groups with no matches auto-drop from the subscription — no need to delete empty groups manually.
commonScenarios.s3Goal: Some nodes carry markers like 'TEST', '测试', 'DEAD' etc. Auto-exclude them from subscriptions to keep users from connecting to broken ones.
commonScenarios.s3Config: Declare exclude-filter on the proxy group. Use a regex to exclude keywords. filter and exclude-filter can be used together — filter pulls in, exclude-filter pushes out.
commonScenarios.s3Effect: Any node whose name contains 'test', 'TEST', '测试', 'DEAD', '故障' etc. won't appear in this group. New nodes get the same check automatically.
commonScenarios.s4Title: Scenario 4: Filter by protocol type (VLESS only / exclude Shadowsocks)
commonScenarios.s4Goal: Want a group to only contain VLESS nodes, or exclude certain protocols (like SS). Common for performance-based routing.
commonScenarios.s4Config: include-type to pull by protocol (separator: |); exclude-type to drop by protocol. Supports vless / vmess / trojan / ss / hysteria / hysteria2 / tuic / wireguard / anytls etc. Case-insensitive.
commonScenarios.s4Effect: include-type: vless|vmess → group only shows VLESS and VMess nodes; exclude-type: ss|hysteria → group drops SS and Hysteria. Stackable with filter / exclude-filter for multi-layer filtering.
commonScenarios.s5Title: Scenario 5: Chain proxy (client → relay → landing → target)
commonScenarios.s5Goal: Set up chained routing. e.g. HK landing → JP relay → target. Common for bypassing local censorship while keeping the landing node's unlocking capability.
commonScenarios.s5Config: Set dialer-proxy on the landing proxy group with the relay group name. The visual editor has a chain link icon on the right side of each group for quick setup. Once set, traffic from nodes in that group routes through the chosen relay node first.
commonScenarios.s5Effect: User picks a 'HK landing' group node → actual traffic path becomes client → JP relay node → HK landing node → target. Chain hops and layers are unlimited — you can stack as many as you need.
commonScenarios.tip1: include-all + filter: pull everything in, then regex-filter for what you want (e.g. 'only nodes with IPLC in the name')
commonScenarios.tip2: include-all + exclude-filter + exclude-type: pull all → drop unwanted keywords → drop certain protocols
commonScenarios.tip3: Declare custom variables at YAML top level (e.g. hkFilter: 'HK|香港'), reuse the same regex across multiple groups so you change it once
commonScenarios.tip4: url-test/fallback auto-select groups: pair with include-all + filter to auto-pick the fastest node from a region

fullExample.heading: Complete Template Example
fullExample.title: Click to view complete V3 template example

notes.heading: Important Notes
notes.formatDesc: : V3 templates use YAML format, incompatible with V2 (INI format), but can be migrated via the conversion feature
notes.filterRuleDesc: : By default, all nodes are used for matching; this is equivalent to explicitly enabling proxy providers + nodes (include-all)
notes.regexDesc: : filter and exclude-filter use regex syntax; special characters need to be escaped
notes.orderDesc: : Proxy group order affects the final configuration; place frequently used proxy groups first
notes.emptyGroupDesc: : If a proxy group's filter matches no nodes, that proxy group is automatically removed
notes.bindingDesc: : After binding a template, the subscription's rule configuration will be completely overridden by the template

trafficInfo.title: Traffic Information
trafficInfo.description: View current user's traffic usage and subscription validity information

intro.heading: Feature Introduction
intro.desc: The Traffic Information page shows the current logged-in user's traffic usage and subscription validity information, helping users understand their service status.
intro.feature1: Displays total traffic quota, used traffic, remaining traffic

preview.heading: Interface Preview
preview.desc: Here is an example of the Traffic Information page:

probe.title: Probe Server Statistics
probe.desc: Traffic data comes from real-time probe server statistics. Administrators need to configure probe servers in "Probe Management" and bind nodes to their corresponding probes for accurate traffic statistics.

external.title: External Subscription Traffic
external.desc: If the administrator has enabled "Sync External Subscription Traffic Info" in system settings, traffic data will also include traffic information from external subscription sources.

update.title: Version Update
update.description: Multiple ways to update MiaoMiaoWu to the latest version

webUpdate.heroDesc: Starting from version 0.3.5, you can check and update the application directly from the web interface.
webUpdate.howToSteps: Go to "Personal Settings" menu → Click "Check for Updates" button → Confirm update

recentSync.heading: Recent Documentation Sync (2026-04-14)
recentSync.desc: This page has been synced with recent changes from `../miaomiaowu`:

change1.title: Proxy groups support binding relay proxy groups
change1.desc: Proxy group type menu adds "Relay Proxy Group" dropdown configuration, corresponding to chain proxy's

change2.title: Edit proxy groups support column count
change2.desc: Node editing dialog adds 2-6 column switching and remembers last column count. See

change3.title: Bind probe supports search and scroll
change3.desc: Node probe binding dialog adds search input and scroll list. See

change4.title: Default DNS template changed to DoH, Trojan URI defaults to security=none
change4.desc: Corresponding template and subscription conversion behavior has been updated on the backend; generated configurations will reflect more appropriate default values.

changelog.heading: Changelog Display
changelog.desc: When checking for updates, the update dialog renders GitHub Release Notes in rich text format, supporting:
changelog.linksCode: Links & Code: Hyperlinks, inline code highlighting

debugLog.heading: Debug Log
debugLog.desc: Administrators can enable Debug logs in personal settings. When enabled, a floating icon appears on the right side of the page; clicking it opens a real-time log viewer:
debugLog.feature1: • Floating icon on the right side shows runtime duration in real time
debugLog.feature2: • Sidebar viewer scrolls through the latest 200 lines of logs in real time
debugLog.feature3: • Auto-closes after 5 minutes to prevent log files from growing too large
debugLog.feature4: • Automatically downloads log file when closing

webUpdateSteps.heading: Web Update (0.3.5+)
webUpdateSteps.desc: For users who have already installed version 0.3.5 or above:

step1.title: Go to Personal Settings
step1.desc: Click the user avatar in the top right corner, select "Personal Settings"

step2.title: Click Check for Updates
step2.desc: Find the "Check for Updates" button on the settings page and click it

step3.title: Confirm Update
step3.desc: If a new version is available, follow the prompts to confirm and wait for the update to complete

dockerCompose.heading: Docker Compose Update (Recommended)
dockerCompose.desc: For users deployed with Docker Compose, updating is very simple:
dockerCompose.note: Docker Compose automatically handles stopping, removing, and rebuilding the container; data is preserved in mounted volumes.

dockerRun.heading: Docker Run Update
dockerRun.desc: For users deployed with Docker Run, manual steps are required:
dockerRun.note: Please ensure you use the same volume mount paths as the initial installation to preserve data.

scriptUpdate.heading: One-Click Script Update
scriptUpdate.desc: For users installed with the one-click script, use the following command to update:
scriptUpdate.note: The script automatically downloads the latest version and restarts the service.

simpleUpdate.heading: Simple Installation Update
simpleUpdate.desc: For users with simple installation, use the following command to update:
simpleUpdate.note: You need to manually restart the program after the update.

backup.heading: Data Backup Recommendations
backup.important: Important: Back up the data directory before updating in case issues occur during the update process.

directInstall.title: Direct Installation
directInstall.desc: View one-click script and manual installation methods

users.title: User Management
users.description: Manage platform user accounts and subscription assignments (Admin feature)
users.intro: The User Management page is an admin-only feature for managing all platform user accounts, including creating users, editing user information, and assigning subscriptions.

mainFeatures.heading: Main Features
mainFeatures.viewListDesc: : View basic information for all users
mainFeatures.createDesc: : Manually create new user accounts with username and password
mainFeatures.deleteDesc: : Delete user accounts that are no longer needed
mainFeatures.assignSubDesc: : Assign or unassign subscription files to/from users
mainFeatures.remarkDesc: : Add remarks to users for easy identification and management
mainFeatures.customLinkDesc: : Set custom short codes for users to replace auto-generated user short codes, making subscription links easier to remember

createSteps.heading: User Creation Steps
createSteps.usernameDesc: : Set the user's login username (unique, no duplicates)
createSteps.passwordDesc: : Set the user's initial login password
createSteps.remarkDesc: : User remark information for identification and management (optional)
createSteps.step3Desc: You can assign subscriptions during creation or later in "Subscription Management"
createSteps.step4Desc: Click the save button to create the user; the user can then log in with their account

typicalScenario.heading: Typical Use Case
typicalScenario.newUserDesc: When sharing with friends or family: administrator creates an account → assigns the corresponding subscription → notifies the user of their login information.

notes.heading: Important Notes
notes.uniqueDesc: : Usernames must be unique and cannot be changed after creation; please set carefully
notes.deleteDesc: : Deleting a user permanently removes all their data and cannot be recovered
notes.passwordDesc: : Passwords set during user creation should be complex enough, including letters, numbers, and symbols
notes.assignDesc: : Users need at least one assigned subscription to use the service normally

bestPractices.heading: Best Practices
bestPractices.remarkDesc: : Add remark information to users such as "Zhang San's family member", "VIP customer", etc., for easy identification
bestPractices.namingDesc: : Use consistent username naming conventions such as "user001", "vip-zhangsan", etc.
bestPractices.notifyDesc: : After creating a user, promptly share login information (username, password, login URL) with the user
bestPractices.backupDesc: : Regularly export user data for backup
